emails.subject: Concurrent Care Appeal — Auth AUTH-2026-00441 — Termination 2026-04-16
emails.body_text:
EXPEDITED CONCURRENT CARE APPEAL REQUEST

TO: Mojave Crest Assurance Company Appeals & Grievances Department
FROM: Utilization Review Department, Desert Springs Recovery Center
DATE: April 14, 2026
RE: Expedited Appeal - Concurrent Care Authorization Termination

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
MEMBER AND AUTHORIZATION INFORMATION
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Member ID:               MBR-2026-04471
Group ID:                GRP-00892
Authorization ID:        AUTH-2026-00441
Claim ID:                CLM-2026-03917

Authorized Service:      Inpatient Substance Use Disorder Residential Treatment
Facility:                Desert Springs Recovery Center
Facility NPI:            1497263850

Admission Date:          March 18, 2026
Stay Dates:              March 18, 2026 through present (ongoing)
Total Billed Charges:    $88,500.00 (to date)

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TERMINATION NOTICE DETAILS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

MCA Termination Notice Date:     April 13, 2026
Termination Effective Date/Time: April 16, 2026 at 12:00 PM PT
Medical Policy Cited:            MP-018-v2.1-20260101
                                 ("Residential SUD Treatment - Medical Necessity Criteria")

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
BASIS FOR APPEAL
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Desert Springs Recovery Center hereby submits an EXPEDITED APPEAL of the concurrent care authorization termination issued April 13, 2026, on the following grounds:

1. CLINICAL APPROPRIATENESS
   The member continues to meet medical necessity criteria for residential SUD treatment. Clinical deterioration and relapse risk remain elevated. Premature discharge poses immediate threat to member safety and treatment outcomes.

2. MENTAL HEALTH PARITY VIOLATION
   MCA's application of Medical Policy MP-018-v2.1 to residential SUD treatment imposes criteria that are MATERIALLY MORE RESTRICTIVE than those applied to comparable medical/surgical inpatient rehabilitation services, including:

   • Stricter functional improvement timelines
   • More rigid discharge-readiness benchmarks
   • Higher burden of proof for continued stay justification
   • Narrower recognition of co-occurring condition complexity

   This differential standard violates:
   - Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act (MHPAEA), 42 U.S.C. § 300gg-26
   - Nevada Revised Statutes § 695B.255 (Mental Health and Substance Use Disorder Parity)

3. EXPEDITED REVIEW JUSTIFICATION
   With termination effective April 16 at 12:00 PM PT (48 hours from receipt of this appeal), the member faces:
   - Immediate loss of medically necessary care
   - Unsafe discharge during critical treatment phase
   - Increased relapse and hospitalization risk

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
REQUEST
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Desert Springs Recovery Center respectfully requests:

1. EXPEDITED concurrent care appeal review pursuant to NAC 695B.530
2. Extension of authorization coverage pending appeal resolution
3. Independent clinical review by qualified SUD specialist
4. Parity compliance assessment of MP-018-v2.1 application


═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
TREATING PHYSICIAN CERTIFICATION OF EXPEDITED REVIEW NEED
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

I, Dr. Marcus Odera, MD, FASAM, am the attending physician of record for
the member identified above (MBR-2026-04471) at Desert Springs Recovery
Center. I hereby certify, in writing, pursuant to 42 C.F.R. § 438.210 and
Nevada Administrative Code § 695B.530, that application of the standard
internal appeal review timeline would seriously jeopardize this member's
life, health, and ability to regain maximum function.

Clinical Basis for Expedited Certification:
The member is currently enrolled in medically necessary residential SUD
treatment. Authorization has been unilaterally terminated effective
April 16, 2026 at 12:00 PM PT. Premature discharge at this stage of
treatment presents a clinically documented and immediate risk of:

  • Acute relapse with high-lethality overdose potential
  • Medical decompensation secondary to abrupt withdrawal of structured
    therapeutic support
  • Inability to safely transition to a lower level of care within the
    48-hour window imposed by MCA's termination notice

The member cannot safely wait the standard 30-day review window. Expedited
review is medically required. I request that Mojave Crest Assurance Company
honor this certification and process this appeal on an expedited basis
immediately.

Attending Physician:     Dr. Marcus Odera, MD, FASAM
Medical License (NV):    NV-MD-058847
Direct Phone:            (760) 555-0149
Signature Date:          April 14, 2026

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
ATTACHMENTS
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

1. MCA Termination Notice (AUTH-2026-00441)
2. Updated Clinical Progress Notes (through April 13, 2026)
3. Provider Appeal Letter with Detailed Clinical Rationale

Please confirm receipt and provide anticipated timeline for expedited review.

Respectfully submitted,

Utilization Review Department
Desert Springs Recovery Center
NPI: 1497263850
Phone: (760) 555-0147
Fax: (760) 555-0148
ur@desertspringsrecovery.org

emails.subject: Status Inquiry - CASE-2026-00088
emails.body_text:
Appeals Intake Team,

Member Services has received an inquiry from the member below requesting a status update on their pending appeal. Please see forwarded message:

--- FORWARDED MESSAGE ---
From: Member (MBR-2026-17203)
Received: April 11, 2026 9:47 AM

Hello,

I submitted an appeal three weeks ago regarding the denial of my inpatient medical/surgical stay (Case # CASE-2026-00088). I haven't heard anything back and my doctor is asking when we can proceed.

Can you please let me know what the status is and when I can expect a decision?

Thank you
--- END FORWARDED MESSAGE ---

CASE DETAILS:
Case ID: CASE-2026-00088
Member ID: MBR-2026-17203
Appeal Received: March 21, 2026
Service: Inpatient Medical/Surgical Stay

Member Services has logged this inquiry as an inbound contact (Ticket #MS-56721). No new appeal intake action is required.

Please provide status update when available so we can respond to member.

Thanks,
Member Services Team
Mojave Crest Assurance Company
